extracted_rules.condition: If athlete's ACWR is between 0.8 and 1.3
extracted_rules.action: Training load is in the safe zone; proceed with planned sessions; no modification required
extracted_rules.source: ACWR meta-analysis (head_coach_acwr_rules original); Gabbett 2016; CLAUDE.md ACWR section

extracted_rules.rule_name: ACWR caution zone — flag and reduce
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete's ACWR is between 1.3 and 1.5
extracted_rules.action: Flag elevated injury risk to athlete; reduce the next planned session load by 10-15%; do not increase load until ACWR returns to safe zone
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ACWR 1.3–1.5: caution zone; reduce next session load by 10-15%; flag athlete
extracted_rules.source: Gabbett 2016 ACWR injury risk meta-analysis; CLAUDE.md ACWR section

extracted_rules.rule_name: ACWR danger zone — mandatory load reduction
extracted_rules.action: Mandatory significant load reduction; notify athlete of high injury risk; replace planned hard sessions with easy/recovery sessions until ACWR drops below 1.3
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ACWR >1.5: danger zone; mandatory load reduction; replace hard sessions with easy/recovery
extracted_rules.source: Gabbett 2016 ACWR injury risk meta-analysis; CLAUDE.md ACWR section

extracted_rules.condition: When computing ACWR for any athlete
extracted_rules.action: Use EWMA (not simple rolling average) for both acute (7-day) and chronic (28-day) load windows
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ACWR = EWMA_7d_load / EWMA_28d_load; EWMA_n = load_n × λ + EWMA_{n-1} × (1 − λ); λ_acute = 2/(7+1)=0.25; λ_chronic = 2/(28+1)=0.067
extracted_rules.source: Hulin et al. 2016; CLAUDE.md ACWR section

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete trains across multiple sports (hybrid athlete)
extracted_rules.action: Sum TSS-equivalent load across all sports before computing ACWR; do not compute separate ACWRs per sport
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: total_daily_load = Σ(sport_TSS_equivalent); ACWR computed on total_daily_load; run TSS: hours × IF² × 100; cycling TSS: standard
extracted_rules.source: CLAUDE.md ACWR section; strain.py EWMA implementation

extracted_rules.rule_name: 10% weekly total load cap — all sports combined
extracted_rules.condition: Always — when planning the next training week for any athlete
extracted_rules.action: Never increase total weekly load (across all sports) by more than 10% from the previous week
extracted_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Development Rules; consensus across Daniels, Pfitzinger

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete performs trail running, especially with significant elevation change
extracted_rules.action: Apply eccentric load multiplier to TSS; monitor biochemical and neuromuscular markers post-session
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Trail run TSS multiplier: 1.1–1.3× road equivalent (higher on steep descents); monitor CK and RPE next morning
extracted_rules.source: Systematic review: muscle damage in trail running (head_coach_acwr_rules original)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Kinetic variables for lower limb injury monitoring
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete shows ACWR >1.3 AND reports lower limb fatigue or asymmetry
extracted_rules.action: Flag for kinetic variable assessment; specifically monitor eccentric hamstring strength and concentric quad torque; integrate with wellness scores
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Flag: ACWR >1.3 + lower limb fatigue report → request kinetic assessment; eccentric hamstring / concentric quad ratio <0.6 = elevated risk
extracted_rules.source: Systematic review: kinetic variables and lower limb injury in soccer (head_coach_acwr_rules original)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Strength training adherence reduces injury risk
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete participates in contact or high-impact sports
extracted_rules.action: Prescribe and monitor adherence to strength training; consistent strength training reduces sports injury rate
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Consistent strength training adherence: reduces injury rate (meta-analysis effect; RR reduction significant in contact sports)
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: strength training adherence and injury rates in contact sports (head_coach_acwr_rules original)

extracted_rules.condition: When evaluating overall injury risk for any athlete
extracted_rules.action: Integrate ACWR data with contextual indicators: wellness score, RPE trend, sleep quality, and training monotony
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Risk score = f(ACWR, wellness_score, RPE_trend, sleep_hours, training_monotony); flag if ≥2 indicators are elevated simultaneously
extracted_rules.source: Systematic review: integrated injury risk assessment (head_coach_acwr_rules original)
